meta.description: Convert old .avi video to modern .mp4 right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert AVI to MP4 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Change .avi to .mp4 locally in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.
hero.title: Convert AVI to MP4 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop an old .avi file and get a modern .mp4 that plays on phones, the web and current editors. It's converted on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.p1: AVI is an older container — reliable, but bulky and often rejected by phones, browsers and modern editors. MP4 (H.264) is smaller and plays nearly everywhere, so converting both shrinks the file and fixes compatibility.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your clip to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the conversion runs locally in your browser, so nothing is stored or logged. (AVI uses the built-in software engine, so it's a little slower than newer formats.)
faq.a1: No. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — check your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: Why is AVI a bit slower to convert?
faq.a2: AVI isn't handled by the fast hardware path, so it runs through a built-in software encoder in your browser. It still works fully offline — it just takes a little longer than MP4 or MOV.
faq.a3: Usually, yes — MP4 with H.264 is far more efficient than most AVI files, so you often get a smaller file at similar quality. You can also set a target size before converting.
